answer:
I think one of the traits I share with my close friends is a strong sense of loyalty. You know, it's interesting because loyalty seems pretty common among friends, but I feel like it really deepens our connections. For example, whenever one of us is going through a tough time—like a family issue or stress from school—we're always there for each other, ready to lend an ear or offer support. 

I remember when my friend Sam was having a really hard time with his college applications; he just felt so overwhelmed. So, a few of us decided to get together at a café and help him out. We spent hours reviewing his essays and just encouraging him. It's those moments that really highlight how our loyalty manifests in action, and I think it's essential for building trust in our friendships.

Also, I find that this trait creates a safe space for everyone to be themselves without the fear of judgment. We can talk about anything, whether it's personal struggles or just silly stuff, and that openness strengthens our bond. It's definitely not something you find in every friendship, but for us, it's pretty usual. 

In a way, our mutual loyalty not only helps us navigate challenges but also makes our friendships more fulfilling. So, I guess it just reinforces the idea that having loyal friends is invaluable and definitely enriches my life.
title: What trait do you share with your close friends? Is this usual? How does it affect your friendship?
